title: Michał Nowak | SmartRecruiters
text:
Michał Nowak | SmartRecruiters

# Michał Nowak

## Senior Vice President, Engineering

Michał runs SmartRecruiters’ engineering function and is responsible for software development, infrastructure, and security.

Michał spent the last 15 years building distributed systems and managing engineering teams in various industries. He joined SmartRecruiter in 2012 as one of the first employees and helped scale the platform to support the needs of some of the largest enterprises in the world.

He is passionate about creating engineering organizations where teams are empowered to deliver business outcomes.

Michal has a Master’s in Computer Science from Jagiellonian University.

##### Rebecca Carr

##### Thomas DiDesidero

title: Bri Fredriksen — Author at Lever | Lever
text:
Bri Fredriksen — Author at Lever | Lever

# Bri Fredriksen

Content & Social Media Manager

Bri Fredriksen believes good content must be two things: worth reading (not just skimming) and worth acting on. At Employ, she develops content that helps teams navigate hiring challenges and focus on what works, what's next, and what's possible. Her approach blends thoughtful storytelling with a practical understanding of how people read, learn, and make decisions.

## Articles by Bri

14 articles published

AI & Technology Future of Recruiting

## From Credentials to Capability: Why Skills-Based Hiring Is Replacing Resumes

In a recent webinar, Employ’s Michelle Stone and Katy Jenkins sat down with Nathan Mondragon (ProboTalent) and Adriana Gamboa (IBM) to unpack this shift—and share what hiring looks like when resumes are no longer recruiters’ most trusted signal.

June 3, 2026

Future of Recruiting Hiring Strategy

## Across the Generational Divide: A Look at What Different Generations Expect from the Hiring Process

Learn what different generations expect from employers today—from flexibility and communication to AI transparency and hiring experience.

May 27, 2026

AI & Technology Future of Recruiting

## What the Rise in AI-Powered Candidate Fraud Really Means for TA Teams

Candidate fraud is evolving fast. Learn how talent leaders are detecting risk, redefining screening, and building trust into modern hiring.

May 11, 2026

TL; DR If your pipeline is growing, why aren’t your hires improving? Here’s the thing: With fewer qualified candidates and lower screen-to-interview rates, the challenge isn’t attracting more a

title: "Feeling Like a Winner": How ESTO is Humanizing FinTech Recruitment | Teamtailor
text:
"Feeling Like a Winner": How ESTO is Humanizing FinTech Recruitment | Teamtailor

Jan 30, 2026

# "Feeling Like a Winner": How ESTO is Humanizing FinTech Recruitment

Nadezda Muttonen, Regita Kumel, and Triin Kimer, People & Culture Team, ESTO

Discover how Regita Kumel and the ESTO team are proving that lightning-fast recruitment can still feel incredibly personal, creating a 21-day hiring journey where every candidate - hired or not - walks away feeling like a winner.

Aram Winkler

Marketing Manager DACH & Benelux

In the high-stakes world of FinTech, the Baltic company ESTO stands out - not just for its record-breaking revenue growth, but for its approach to the people behind the numbers. Headquartered in Tallinn, ESTO has grown into a 100-person powerhouse by connecting 6,000+ merchants with innovative consumer finance solutions. But as any fast-growth company knows, scaling the business requires scaling the team and culture.

We sat down with Regita Kumel, Talent & Employer Branding Manager at ESTO, to discuss how they moved away from modules that didn't keep up with the companies fast growth, to embrace a recruitment process that is as intuitive and data-driven as the financial products they build.

Talent & Employer Branding Manager, ESTO

## Meet Regita Kumel

Regita is the Talent & Employer Branding Manager at ESTO, bringing a wealth of experience from both HR agencies and in-house roles at high-growth companies. Specializing in the Fintech, e-commerce, and service sectors, she focuses on bridging the gap between strategic recruitment and authentic employer branding.

### The Need for a Centralized Hub

Teamtailor: Before Teamtailor, what were the "growing pains" in your recruitment process?

Regita: We were using a basic recruitment module, but it lacked the centralization we needed. Managers couldn’t independently track their own processes, and we didn’t have a way to build a long-term talent pool. We needed a platform that was flexible and intuitive - s

title: Dara Brenner — Author at Lever | Lever
text:
Dara Brenner — Author at Lever | Lever

# Dara Brenner

Chief Product Officer, Employ

Dara Brenner is the Chief Product Officer of Employ Inc. who brings more than 25 years of executive leadership experience spanning product vision, business strategy, and organizational excellence. She has a proven track record of delivering customer-centric solutions, guiding companies through transformative growth, and building future-ready operating models. As Chief Product Officer at Employ, she has spearheaded a major evolution of the company’s product portfolio, including the integration of AI capabilities. Under her leadership, Dara set a new standard for HR technology, advancing the product strategy and delivering solutions that redefined how employers and job seekers connect. Dara has held senior leadership positions at ADP, Equifax Workforce Solutions, and Ultimate Software/UKG, where she shaped strategic direction, drove operational advancements, and delivered industry-leading workforce technology. Notably, she founded and scaled ADP’s first Innovation Lab, pioneering the adoption of agile practices that reshaped how teams collaborated and accelerated delivery of forward-looking solutions across the organization. As CEO of Employ, Dara is committed to advancing the company’s mission to reshape the hiring experience. She is passionate about driving product evolution, enabling long-term customer success, and ensuring that Employ continues to lead with innovation, integrity, and impact in the HR technology space.

## Articles by Dara

1 article published

AI & Technology ATS

## From Chaos to Clarity to Connection: How Lever’s Spring 2026 Release Puts the Human Back in Hiring

Learn how Lever’s Spring 2026 release helps you reduce hiring noise, strengthen candidate signal, and focus on real connections.

May 7, 2026

title: Jonathan Milne — Author at Lever | Lever
text:
Jonathan Milne — Author at Lever | Lever

JM

# Jonathan Milne

Jonathan Milne is the VP of AI Services at Demand Spring, where he helps B2B marketing teams put smart strategies and automation tools into action—from Marketo to HubSpot to Salesforce. With 20+ years in tech, Jon got his start in HR software at emPerform, giving him a unique perspective on the intersection of marketing, AI, and talent technology. As recognized thought leader, Jon has spoken at top industry events like TiECon Canada, Accelerate OTT, MarketingProfs B2B Forum, and B2B Marketing Exchange. He’s also been named a Top 40 Under 40 honoree by the Ottawa Business Journal and is the founder of the AI for Good Forum, a community initiative helping professionals use AI to grow their careers and make a positive impact.

## Articles by Jonathan

No articles published yet.

title: How Knauf transformed its global recruitment with Teamtailor
text:
How Knauf transformed its global recruitment with Teamtailor

Feb 26, 2026

# How Knauf Transformed its Global Recruitment for 43,000 Employees

Bridging tradition and technology: The Global Recruitment team is bringing Knauf’s 90-year history into the digital age

Discover how James Forster, Global Recruitment Lead at Knauf, steered a two-year digital transformation to unify recruitment for 43,500 employees, ultimately driving a 50% increase in applicant volume while bridging the gap between a 90-year legacy and modern talent acquisition.

Aram Winkler

Marketing Manager DACH & Benelux

Knauf is a global leader in the building materials industry - a 90-year-old, family-owned powerhouse. But as their factories became "smarter" and their sales processes went digital, their recruitment process faced a challenge: it was stuck in a decentralized, manual past.

We spoke with James Forster, Global Recruitment Lead at the Knauf Group People Team, about how he navigated this complex transformation. His goal? To move from a fragmented "black box" of local processes to a unified, "best-in-class" global engine.

## Why Change was needed

Question: How would you describe Knauf’s recruitment philosophy? In an industry as established as yours, how do you balance "tradition" with the need for modern talent acquisition?

James Forster: "Our recruitment philosophy centres on two pillars: we deliver a best-in-class candidate experience—clear, timely communication; transparency, fair, inclusive evaluation; and respectful closure for every applicant. We also equip our people with a robust, data-enabled hiring system that empowers both candidates and colleagues to identify, assess, and secure best-in-class talent, consistently and at scale. Historically one of the last to embrace digitalisation, our sector is now moving quickly. We seek people who are technologically fluent but also thrive within a family-owned, values-led environment. Our process should mirror the organisation we are b

EnableComp Case Study: 300+ Annual Hires with Lever | Lever

Healthcare

# How EnableComp Built a Scalable Recruiting Engine With Lever

EnableComp built a scalable, data-driven recruiting engine with Lever, supporting 300+ hires annually with structured, collaborative hiring processes designed for growth.

300+

Hires supported annually

50+

Hires managed in high-volume bursts

1

Centralized hiring platform

## The Challenge

Before Lever, recruiting at EnableComp felt fragmented. Processes lived across spreadsheets, inboxes, and side conversations, making it difficult for the team to maintain visibility or momentum. Candidate stages weren't centralized, feedback lived in email threads, and reporting required pulling data from multiple sources.

"Recruiting often felt reactive and manual — like we were constantly playing catch-up," says Donnie. Without a stronger foundation, growth would have meant adding more manual work rather than improving efficiency.

EnableComp didn't just need a new ATS. They needed recruiting infrastructure designed for intentional growth.

## The Solution

After evaluating multiple platforms, EnableComp chose Lever for its flexibility, collaboration tools, and intuitive user experience. Recruiting operations quickly moved from disconnected systems into a single, centralized platform.

Key capabilities included:

- Customizable workflows aligned to EnableComp's hiring stages
- Centralized feedback and communication
- Real-time pipeline visibility and reporting
- An intuitive interface that accelerated team adoption

## The Results

### Increased Productivity

Lever eliminated significant manual work and back-and-forth communication. Recruiters and hiring managers now spend less time tracking updates and more time having meaningful conversations with candidates. Structured workflows reduce delays, helping roles fill faster.

### Leadership Confidence Through Data

Instead of relying on anecdotal updates, leadership now has clear visibility

title: How Pemo scaled fintech hiring in MENA | Teamtailor
text:
How Pemo scaled fintech hiring in MENA | Teamtailor

Apr 7, 2026

# How Pemo scaled fintech hiring in MENA

Louise Cross

UK Marketing at Teamtailor

The fintech world moves fast. Pemo, a B2B corporate finance platform serving the MENA region, knew they had to match that pace when it came to hiring. But speed alone wasn't enough. They needed to build something sustainable: a hiring function that could scale without sacrificing candidate experience or quality of hire.

Before adopting Teamtailor, Pemo was managing recruitment the way many growing companies do: shared spreadsheets, scattered email threads, and candidate information spread across multiple platforms. There was no centralized system, no pipeline visibility, and no real employer brand to speak of. It worked well at first and as the company expanded across the UAE and opened their Cairo office, new opportunities for improvement and growth began to emerge.

The real challenge wasn't just volume. It was the unique complexity of fintech hiring in the MENA region: competing for a relatively small pool of talent with both technical depth and regional market knowledge, navigating cross-cultural hiring across multiple countries and the trust-based screening required in financial services. High-quality candidates in this space don't stay on the market long, and they're evaluating employers just as rigorously as employers evaluate them.

That's when Pemo decided to invest in a proper foundation for their recruitment process.

People & Talent Lead @ Pemo

## Meet Dina Ismail

Dina owns People & Talent Management at Pemo, driving the hiring strategy across regions and acting as a People Business Partner to support teams. This includes leading end-to-end recruitment, partnering with leaders on workforce planning and hiring decisions, managing people and talent partners, and supporting employee relations and capability building. She focuses on attracting high-quality talent and creating experiences that enable people t

# Michał Nowak

## Senior VP, Technik

Michał leitet die technische Abteilung von SmartRecruiters und ist verantwortlich für Softwareentwicklung, Infrastruktur und Sicherheit.

Michał hat die letzten 15 Jahre damit verbracht, verteilte Systeme aufzubauen und Ingenieurteams in verschiedenen Branchen zu leiten. Er kam 2012 als einer der ersten Mitarbeiter zu SmartRecruiter und half bei der Skalierung der Plattform, um die Anforderungen einiger der größten Unternehmen der Welt zu erfüllen.

Seine Leidenschaft ist es, technische Organisationen zu schaffen, in denen Teams befähigt werden, Geschäftsergebnisse zu erzielen.

Michal hat einen Master-Abschluss in Informatik von der Jagiellonen-Universität.

title: How Knauf transformed its global recruitment with Teamtailor
text:
How Knauf transformed its global recruitment with Teamtailor

 

 

26 feb 2026

Klantverhalen

# How Knauf Transformed its Global Recruitment for 43,000 Employees

Bridging tradition and technology: The Global Recruitment team is bringing Knauf’s 90-year history into the digital age

Discover how James Forster, Global Recruitment Lead at Knauf, steered a two-year digital transformation to unify recruitment for 43,500 employees, ultimately driving a 50% increase in applicant volume while bridging the gap between a 90-year legacy and modern talent acquisition.

Aram Winkler

Marketing Manager DACH & BENELUX

Knauf is a global leader in the building materials industry - a 90-year-old, family-owned powerhouse. But as their factories became "smarter" and their sales processes went digital, their recruitment process faced a challenge: it was stuck in a decentralized, manual past.

We spoke with James Forster, Global Recruitment Lead at the Knauf Group People Team, about how he navigated this complex transformation. His goal? To move from a fragmented "black box" of local processes to a unified, "best-in-class" global engine.

## Why Change was needed

Question: How would you describe Knauf’s recruitment philosophy? In an industry as established as yours, how do you balance "tradition" with the need for modern talent acquisition?

James Forster: "Our recruitment philosophy centres on two pillars: we deliver a best-in-class candidate experience—clear, timely communication; transparency, fair, inclusive evaluation; and respectful closure for every applicant. We also equip our people with a robust, data-enabled hiring system that empowers both candidates and colleagues to identify, assess, and secure best-in-class talent, consistently and at scale. Historically one of the last to embrace digitalisation, our sector is now moving quickly. We seek people who are technologically fluent but also thrive within a family-owned, values-led environment. Our process should mirror the o
